mnemonics.title: Kruskal's Steps
mnemonics.content: S.O.R.T. - Sort edges, Initialize Union-Find, Repeat picking minimum edge, Terminate when V-1 edges added

mnemonics.title: Greedy Strategy
mnemonics.content: Greedy Kruskal Says: Take Minimum, Avoid Cycles - always pick the smallest edge that doesn't form a loop

acronyms.usage: The output of Kruskal's Algorithm - a tree connecting all vertices with minimum total weight
Also called Union-Find - data structure used to detect cycles in Kruskal's Algorithm
Time complexity of Kruskal's Algorithm due to sorting edges

visualTips.visualization: Imagine sorting coins (edges) by weight, then picking coins one by one while ensuring you don't pick two coins that connect the same already-connected group of vertices
Think of islands (vertices) connected by bridges (edges). Union-Find tracks which islands are already connected. When you want to add a bridge, check if it connects two separate island groups.
Picture building a minimum-cost network - like laying cables. Always pick the cheapest cable that doesn't create a redundant loop in the existing network.

analogies.analogy: Kruskal's is like building roads between cities by always selecting the cheapest road that doesn't create a closed loop - you start with nothing and add roads one by one.
Like a network engineer laying cables - always choose the shortest cable that connects a new location, avoiding duplicate connections.
Union-Find is like tracking family groups - Find checks which family someone belongs to, Union merges two families, and we avoid connecting people from the same family (which would create a cycle).
